signal_title: Executive Order 14117: Preventing Access to Americans’ Sensitive Data by Countries of Concern
risk_rationale: The executive order prohibits the transfer of Americans' sensitive data to certain foreign countries, which may conflict with TikTok's current data residency and cross-border data transfer policies.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prohibits data transfer to countries of concern
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline allows for data transfers under specific frameworks, but the executive order prohibits transfers to certain countries, potentially conflicting with existing mechanisms.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Employee access to US data limited to US-based personnel
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Mandates DOJ to establish regulations preventing data access
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The executive order may require additional restrictions or changes to current access controls to comply with new DOJ regulations.

remediations.title: Review and Update Data Transfer Policies
remediations.description: Conduct a thorough review of current data transfer policies and align them with the new executive order to ensure compliance with prohibitions on data transfers to countries of concern.
acceptance_criteria: Updated data transfer policies are documented and approved
No data transfers occur to prohibited countries

remediations.title: Enhance Access Control Measures
remediations.description: Implement additional access control measures as per DOJ regulations to prevent unauthorized access to US data by foreign entities.
acceptance_criteria: Access control measures are updated and verified
Compliance with DOJ regulations is documented

business_impact: Potential disruption in data operations and increased compliance costs due to the need to modify data transfer and access control policies.
recommended_actions: Engage with legal and policy teams to interpret the executive order's implications
Initiate a compliance project to update data transfer and access control policies
Monitor DOJ's regulatory developments for further guidance
